title: Stdlib: Plane, Axis, BoundingBox structures + constructors
analysis: The task adds three new geometric types (Plane, Axis, BoundingBox) to the Reify type system and stdlib. The codebase has well-established patterns from recently-added composite types Frame(origin+basis) and Transform(rotation+translation). Each new type needs: (1) a Type enum variant in ty.rs with Display and factory method, (2) a Value enum variant in value.rs with PartialEq, Ord, Display, content_hash, and dimension trait impls, and (3) constructor functions in eval_builtin (stdlib/lib.rs). The task specifies 6 convenience constructors (plane_xy/xz/yz with offset, axis_x/y/z with origin point) plus BoundingBox needs a bbox(min,max) constructor with computed size/center accessor functions (bbox_size, bbox_center). The existing type_tag numbering goes up to 21 (Transform); new types get 22-24. Content hash tags similarly extend from 21 to 24. Value::Point/Vector are the established building blocks — Plane stores origin as Point3 and normal as Vector3<Dimensionless>, Axis stores origin as Point3 and direction as Vector3<Dimensionless>, BoundingBox stores min/max as Point3 pairs. The tensor_components_f64 helper works on Point/Vector values (confirmed by normalize/magnitude usage) and will be reused for BoundingBox computed fields.

prerequisites.description: Add Type::Plane, Type::Axis, Type::BoundingBox variants to ty.rs enum. Add Display arms ('Plane', 'Axis', 'BoundingBox'). Add factory methods: Type::plane(), Type::axis(), Type::bounding_box().

Add Value::Plane { origin: Box<Value>, normal: Box<Value> } variant to value.rs. Implement: PartialEq (delegate to inner eq), Ord (type_tag=22, compare origin then normal), Display ('plane(origin, normal)'), content_hash (tag=22, combine origin+normal hashes), dimension (returns DIMENSIONLESS following Frame/Transform convention). Add tests: construction, equality, display, content_hash determinism, content_hash no-collision with Transform(tag=21), Ord cross-type ordering.

Add Value::Axis { origin: Box<Value>, direction: Box<Value> } variant to value.rs. Implement: PartialEq (delegate), Ord (type_tag=23, compare origin then direction), Display ('axis(origin, direction)'), content_hash (tag=23), dimension (DIMENSIONLESS). Add tests following same pattern as Plane.

Add Value::BoundingBox { min: Box<Value>, max: Box<Value> } variant to value.rs. Implement: PartialEq (delegate), Ord (type_tag=24, compare min then max), Display ('bbox(min, max)'), content_hash (tag=24), dimension (DIMENSIONLESS). Add tests following same pattern.

steps.description: Write failing tests for plane_xy(offset): (1) plane_xy(5mm) returns Plane with origin=Point(0m,0m,5mm) and normal=Vector(0,0,1), (2) plane_xy() with no args returns Undef, (3) plane_xy(Bool) returns Undef, (4) plane_xy with 2 args returns Undef, (5) plane_xy(NaN) returns Undef, (6) plane_xy(Inf) returns Undef, (7) plane_xy(Real(0.0)) produces dimensionless origin.

Implement plane_xy constructor in eval_builtin: validate single numeric arg via as_f64(), reject NaN/Inf, extract dimension, build origin Point3 with (0,0,offset) using matching dimension for zero-components, build normal Vector3(0,0,1) as dimensionless Reals, return Value::Plane{origin, normal}.

Write failing tests for plane_xz(offset) and plane_yz(offset): (1) plane_xz(3mm) produces origin=(0m,3mm,0m) and normal=(0,1,0), (2) plane_yz(7mm) produces origin=(7mm,0m,0m) and normal=(1,0,0), (3) plane_xz/yz with invalid args produce Undef.

Implement plane_xz and plane_yz constructors in eval_builtin. Extract shared helper make_plane(args, offset_index, normal_components) to DRY the three plane constructors. plane_xz: offset at y-position, normal=(0,1,0). plane_yz: offset at x-position, normal=(1,0,0). Refactor plane_xy to use the shared helper.

Write failing tests for axis_z(origin): (1) axis_z(Point3<Length>) returns Axis with passed origin and direction=Vector(0,0,1), (2) axis_z with no args returns Undef, (3) axis_z(Real) returns Undef (not a Point), (4) axis_z(Point2) returns Undef (wrong dimensionality), (5) axis_z(Vector3) returns Undef (must be Point not Vector).
Implement axis_z constructor in eval_builtin: validate single arg is Value::Point with 3 components, build direction Vector3(0,0,1) as dimensionless Reals, return Value::Axis{origin, direction}.

Write failing tests for axis_x and axis_y: (1) axis_x(origin) returns direction=(1,0,0), (2) axis_y(origin) returns direction=(0,1,0), (3) axis_x/y with invalid args return Undef, (4) axis constructors with dimensionless Point3 origin.

Implement axis_x and axis_y constructors. Extract shared helper make_axis(args, direction_components) to DRY the three axis constructors. axis_x: direction=(1,0,0). axis_y: direction=(0,1,0). Refactor axis_z to use the shared helper.

Write failing tests for bbox(min, max): (1) bbox with two Point3<Length> values returns BoundingBox, (2) bbox with mismatched dimensions returns Undef, (3) bbox with non-Point args returns Undef, (4) bbox with Point2 returns Undef, (5) bbox with wrong arg count returns Undef, (6) bbox with one Point and one Vector returns Undef.
Implement bbox constructor in eval_builtin: validate exactly 2 args, both Value::Point with 3 components, matching dimensions. Return Value::BoundingBox{min, max}.

Write failing tests for BoundingBox computed fields: (1) bbox_size on BoundingBox with min=(1m,2m,3m) max=(4m,6m,9m) returns Vector(3m,4m,6m), (2) bbox_center on same returns Point(2.5m,4m,6m), (3) bbox_size/bbox_center with non-BoundingBox arg returns Undef, (4) bbox_size/bbox_center with wrong arg count returns Undef, (5) bbox_size on dimensionless BoundingBox returns Vector of Reals.

Implement bbox_size and bbox_center in eval_builtin. bbox_size: extract min/max via tensor_components_f64, compute component-wise (max-min), return Vector3 with same dimension. bbox_center: compute (min+max)/2.0 component-wise, return Point3 with same dimension. Use dimension-aware make_component helper for Value construction.

design_decisions.decision: Use Value::Point for origins (Plane, Axis) and Value::Point for BoundingBox min/max, consistent with Frame{origin: Point, ...} convention
design_decisions.rationale: The task description says 'origin Vector<Length>' but the established codebase convention (Frame, Transform) uses Point for spatial origins and Vector for displacements. Points and Vectors have distinct semantics (point+vector=point, point-point=vector). Following the existing convention ensures consistency and correct arithmetic behavior downstream.

design_decisions.decision: Store BoundingBox as just min+max; compute size and center via bbox_size/bbox_center stdlib functions rather than eager storage or method dispatch
design_decisions.rationale: Keeps the Value variant minimal (2 fields vs 4), avoids redundant data, and stays within the task's module scope (reify-stdlib + reify-types). Method dispatch (.size, .center) would require changes to reify-expr which is outside scope. Stdlib accessor functions follow the existing functional pattern.

design_decisions.decision: Content hash tags: Plane=22, Axis=23, BoundingBox=24. Ord type tags: Plane=22, Axis=23, BoundingBox=24
design_decisions.rationale: Extends sequentially from the highest existing tag (Transform=21). Maintains unique tags for each variant to prevent hash collisions and ensure deterministic ordering.

design_decisions.decision: Plane constructors accept any numeric offset (Int, Real, Scalar) via as_f64(). Zero-components in origin match the offset's dimension.
design_decisions.rationale: Follows the existing pattern where construct_point_or_vector accepts Int/Real/Scalar. If offset is Scalar{5mm, LENGTH}, origin zeros are Scalar{0, LENGTH}. If offset is Real(5.0), zeros are Real(0.0). This preserves dimension consistency within the Point.

design_decisions.decision: Axis constructors require Point3 origin (not Tensor or Vector), validated via Value::Point match with components.len()==3
design_decisions.rationale: Consistent with Frame constructor which requires Point3 for origin. Using a Point (not Vector) communicates the correct geometric semantics. Rejecting Vector/Tensor prevents accidental misuse.

design_decisions.decision: Normal vectors and direction vectors are always dimensionless (Value::Real components), not dependent on origin dimension
design_decisions.rationale: Unit direction vectors are inherently dimensionless — they represent direction, not magnitude. This matches CAD convention (gp_Dir in OCCT is unit-length and dimensionless) and avoids dimension complexity in downstream geometry operations.

design_decisions.decision: Extract shared helpers make_plane() and make_axis() to DRY the three plane_xy/xz/yz and axis_x/y/z constructors
design_decisions.rationale: The three plane constructors differ only in which component gets the offset and which normal vector is used. Similarly for axes. A shared helper eliminates code duplication and ensures consistent validation across all variants.

reuse.where: crates/reify-types/src/value.rs lines 62-64 (Frame, Transform variants) and their trait impls (PartialEq ~420-427, Ord ~593-601, Display ~732-737, content_hash ~324-331)
reuse.how: Copy the pattern exactly for Plane, Axis, BoundingBox: Box<Value> fields, delegating PartialEq/Ord to inner values, combining content hashes with unique tags, Display as 'name(field1, field2)'

Reference the dimension-matching and numeric-validation pattern when implementing plane offset validation. Don't call directly (planes aren't simple point/vector constructors) but follow the same validation approach.

reuse.where: crates/reify-stdlib/src/lib.rs (used by normalize, magnitude, cross, dot)
reuse.how: Call directly in bbox_size and bbox_center to extract f64 components and dimension from BoundingBox min/max Points. The helper already handles Point and Vector values.

Use in plane constructors to create zero-valued Scalar components with matching dimension: Value::Scalar { si_value: 0.0, dimension: offset_dim } when offset is dimensioned, or Value::Real(0.0) when dimensionless.
Use as_f64() + is_finite() check in plane constructors to reject NaN/Inf offsets early, following the existing convention that non-finite values become Undef.
Mirror the exact validation structure for axis constructors: check arg count, validate first arg is Value::Point with 3 components, validate/construct second field, return composite Value.
